skill.name: github-repo-search
skill.description: 帮助用户搜索和筛选 GitHub 开源项目，输出结构化推荐报告。当用户说"帮我找开源项目"、"搜一下GitHub上有什么"、"找找XX方向的仓库"、"开源项目推荐"、"github搜索"、"/github-search"时触发。
skill.summary: GitHub 开源项目搜索助手，通过结构化工作流帮助用户找到可理解、可比较、可决策的候选仓库列表
skill.author: yunshu0909

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 35 potential issues (32 external commands, 2 weak crypto, 1 high entropy). All findings are FALSE POSITIVES. The SKILL.md file is pure Markdown documentation without any executable code. Backtick patterns are Markdown code delimiters, not shell execution. No cryptographic algorithms or encrypted content present. The skill is a legitimate GitHub repository search workflow guide.

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Delimiters
dangerous_patterns.description: Static scanner flagged backtick characters (`) as Ruby/shell backtick execution. This is incorrect. The file is pure Markdown where backticks are used for code formatting, not command execution. No shell commands are executed by this skill.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: SKILL.md contains only Markdown documentation text. Backticks appear in code fence blocks and inline code formatting, not as Ruby/shell command operators.

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive: YAML Field Names
dangerous_patterns.description: Static scanner flagged 'description' and 'archived' fields as 'weak cryptographic algorithm'. This is incorrect. These are legitimate YAML frontmatter and data field names used for repository metadata.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is YAML frontmatter description field. Line 110 is a list showing repository data fields. No cryptographic algorithms present.

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive: File Header Entropy
dangerous_patterns.description: Static scanner flagged high entropy at line 1. Line 1 is the YAML frontmatter opening delimiter (---). The file is plain text Markdown, not binary or encrypted.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: File is readable plain text Markdown. The YAML delimiter (---) at line 1 is standard formatting, not encrypted content.

content.user_title: Search GitHub Repositories with AI-Powered Insights
content.value_statement: Finding the right open-source project on GitHub is overwhelming with millions of repositories. This skill uses structured workflows to search, filter, and rank repositories, delivering actionable recommendations you can use immediately.
actual_capabilities: Search GitHub using natural language queries with multiple search strategies
Filter results by stars, language, update time, and repository type
Classify repositories into categories (framework, application, tooling)
Generate structured reports with detailed project introductions
Rank results by relevance, activity, and maturity
Iterate search results based on user feedback
limitations: Only searches public GitHub repositories (no private repos)
Requires user confirmation on search requirements before execution
Does not include installation or implementation guidance

use_cases.title: Research Open Source Tools for a Project
use_cases.description: When starting a new project, quickly find and compare relevant open source libraries and frameworks with structured recommendations.

use_cases.title: Discover Trending Technologies
use_cases.description: Explore popular repositories in emerging technology areas like AI agents, RAG systems, or browser automation.

use_cases.title: Learn About New Technology Stacks
use_cases.description: Find well-maintained open source projects to study and understand best practices in a specific domain.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Repository Search
prompt_templates.prompt: 帮我找开源项目，关于机器学习模型部署的
prompt_templates.scenario: Simple request to find ML deployment repositories

prompt_templates.title: Specific Technology Search
prompt_templates.prompt: 我想找 Python 的 Web 框架，要活跃维护的，至少 1000 stars
prompt_templates.scenario: Search with specific language and quality requirements

prompt_templates.title: Learning Resources Search
prompt_templates.prompt: 帮我找学习资料，关于微服务架构的，优先选有详细文档的
prompt_templates.scenario: Finding learning-oriented repositories with good documentation

prompt_templates.title: Competitive Analysis Search
prompt_templates.prompt: 我要做一个类似 Notion 的应用，帮我找相关开源实现参考，Top 20
prompt_templates.scenario: Finding reference implementations for a product idea

output_examples.input: 帮我找 React 状态管理相关的开源项目
output_examples.output:
Top 5 React State Management Repositories:

1. Redux (meta/redux) - 60k stars
   Type: Framework Layer
   What it is: Predictable state container for JavaScript apps
   Why recommend: Industry standard with extensive ecosystem

2. Zustand (pmndrs/zustand) - 35k stars
   Type: Application Layer
   What it is: Minimal state management with hooks API
   Why recommend: Lightweight alternative with excellent DX

output_examples.input: 搜索 Rust Web 框架
output_examples.output:
Rust Web Framework Comparison:

1. Axum (tokio-rs/axum) - Web framework
2. Actix-web (actix/actix-web) - Actor-based framework
3. Rocket (rocket-org/rocket) - Rust-focused batteries-included

best_practices: Confirm search requirements with the skill before execution to ensure relevant results
Start with broad searches and refine based on initial results
Use the ranked report to prioritize evaluation based on your specific needs
anti_patterns: Do not skip the requirement confirmation step, even if your request seems clear
Avoid requesting too many results at once; start with Top 10 and iterate
Do not mix installation/implementation questions with the search workflow

faq.question: Does this skill access my private GitHub repositories?
faq.answer: No. This skill only searches public GitHub repositories by default. Private repository access requires authentication which is not included.

faq.question: How does the ranking work?
faq.answer: Results are ranked by a weighted combination of relevance (35%), use case fit (30%), activity (15%), maturity (15%), and stars (5%).

faq.question: Can I search for repositories in a specific programming language?
faq.answer: Yes. You can specify language requirements in your search request. The skill will filter and rank accordingly.

faq.question: What is the difference between the repository types?
faq.answer: Types include: Framework (for building on), Application (ready-to-use products), Tooling (utilities), and Documentation (learning resources).

faq.question: How current are the search results?
faq.answer: Results are based on real-time GitHub search with timestamps. The report includes retrieval time and GitHub API rate limit status.

faq.question: Can I get help with installing a found repository?
faq.answer: The search workflow focuses on discovery and evaluation. Installation and implementation are separate processes not included in this skill.
